5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>OpenAPI spec</a></div></div></div><p>Insurance discovery checks search for a patient&#x27;s active coverage using only their demographic data.</p>
<ol>
<li>Call this endpoint with as much <a href="/docs/healthcare/insurance-discovery#required-patient-information">patient demographic information</a> as possible.</li>
<li>Stedi searches for active coverage for the patient.</li>
<li>The endpoint returns an array of potential active coverages along with subscriber details and benefits information.</li>
</ol>
<p>We recommend using insurance discovery checks as a backup when eligibility checks fail or aren&#x27;t possible. Because of their limitations, you shouldn&#x27;t rely on them as your primary method for verifying patient coverage.</p><div class="flex items-end justify-between gap-2"><h2 class="flex scroll-m-28 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Demographic information for the patient when they are a dependent on a health plan.</p>
<ul>
<li>We <strong>strongly recommend</strong> providing as much information as possible to improve the probability of finding matching coverage. We especially recommend providing the dependent&#x27;s Social Security Number and their address - particularly their zip code.</li>
<li>You should provide information for both the subscriber and the dependent in the request when possible.</li>
<li>If you only have the dependent&#x27;s information, you should identify them in the <code>subscriber</code> object instead and leave this object empty. Note that some payers require information about both the dependent and the subscriber, so providing only the dependent&#x27;s information limits Stedi&#x27;s ability to return coverage matches for those payers.</li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="body.dependent" empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The dependent&#x27;s address. We strongly recommend providing this information if possible to improve the probability of finding matching coverage.</p>
<p>We especially recommend providing the patient’s ZIP code, as this helps narrow down the list of probable payers. Zip code search isn’t an exact match, so even the first 3-4 digits of the patient’s current zip code can help improve the results. If the patient’s current address isn’t available, you can try a full or partial zip code from one of the patient’s previous addresses or even one in close proximity.</p><details are the health plan subscriber. We strongly recommend providing as much information as possible to improve the probability of finding matching coverage.</p>
<p>We especially recommend providing the subscriber&#x27;s Social Security Number and their address - particularly their zip code.</p><details 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The subscriber&#x27;s current or previous address. We strongly recommend providing this information if possible to improve the probability of finding matching coverage.</p>
<p>We especially recommend providing the patient’s ZIP code, as this helps narrow down the list of probable payers. Zip code search isn’t an exact match, so even the first 3-4 digits of the patient’s current zip code can help improve the results. If the patient’s current address isn’t available, you can try a full or partial zip code from one of the patient’s previous addresses or even one in close proximity.</p><details class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank"><code>AAA</code> errors</a> that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions.</p>
<p>Any errors that occur at the <code>payer</code>, <code>provider</code>, <code>subscriber</code>, or <code>dependents</code> levels are also included in this array, allowing you to review all errors in a central location. If there are no <code>AAA</code> errors, this array will be empty.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none leading-[24px]"><p>An array of potential coverage matches for the patient. This will only be populated if the insurance discovery check <code>status</code> is <code>COMPLETE</code>. Each item in the array contains information about a potential match, including the provider, subscriber, payer, and plan information.</p><details 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the patient&#x27;s healthcare benefits, such as coverage level (individual vs. family), coverage type (deductibles, copays, etc.), out of pocket maximums, and more. This is the same information you would get from a standard eligibility check.</p>
<p>Payers typically return at least the following properties: <code>code</code>, <code>coverageLevelCode</code>, <code>serviceTypeCodes</code>, and either <code>benefitAmount</code> or <code>benefitPercent</code>. However, the exact properties returned in this object are up to the payer&#x27;s discretion.</p>
<p>Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-active-coverage-benefits" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Determine patient benefits</a> in our eligibility check documentation for more information about benefit types, details about how to interpret the response, and additional examples.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating whether the benefit is subject to prior authorization or certification. Can be <code>Y</code> - Yes, <code>N</code> - No, or <code>U</code> - Unknown.</p>
<ul>
<li>When this property is set to <code>U</code>, it means the payer can&#x27;t determine in real time whether prior authorization is required for the service. They may require additional details, like diagnosis or place of service to make a determination. Check the <code>additionalInformation.description</code> property for any additional clarification. You can also contact the payer directly or use their provider portal to get more information.</li>
<li>Some payers may send additional notes about prior authorization rules in the <code>additionalInformation.description</code> property. Payers may also send prior authorization notes in a separate <code>benefitsInformation</code> object with a code of <code>1</code> (Active coverage), <code>CB</code> (Coverage Benefit), or <code>D</code> (Benefit Description).</li>
</ul>
<p>Visit our <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-network-status-authorization-referrals#is-prior-authorization-required" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">patient benefits docs</a> to learn more about prior authorization.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>N</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>U</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>Y</code></div></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3" 14.8284L14.8282 7.75736Z"></path></svg></span></span></a><div class="text-[12px] font-[400] text-tertiary font-mono break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].benefitAmount</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The monetary benefit amount, such as a patient&#x27;s co-pay or deductible. This value is expressed as a decimal, such as 100.00.</p>
<p>The payer will always send a value in this property when the <code>benefitsInformation.code</code> = <code>B</code> - Co-Payment, <code>C</code> - Deductible, <code>G</code> - Out of Pocket (Stop Loss), <code>J</code> - Cost Containment, or <code>Y</code> - Spend Down. For those codes, this value represents the patient&#x27;s portion of responsibility.</p>
<p>The payer will <strong>never</strong> send this value when <code>benefitsInformation.code</code> = <code>A</code> - Co-Insurance. This property can contain zero when the patient has no responsibility.</p>
<p>Learn more about <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-patient-responsibility-benefits" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">patient costs</a>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3" id="response.items.benefitsInformation.benefitPercent"><div class="flex flex-col gap-2 break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].benefitPercent</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The percentage of the benefit, such as co-insurance. This property can contain zero when the patient has no responsibility.</p>
<p>The payer will always send a value in this property when <code>benefitsInformation.code</code> = <code>A</code> - Co-Insurance. For this code, this value represents the patient&#x27;s portion of the responsibility. The percentage is expressed as a decimal, such as <code>0.80</code> represents 80%.</p>
<p>The payer will <strong>never</strong> send a value in this property when <code>benefitsInformation.code</code> = <code>B</code> - Co-Payment, <code>C</code> - Deductible, <code>G</code> - Out of Pocket (Stop Loss), <code>J</code> - Cost Containment, or <code>Y</code> - Spend Down.</p>
<p>Learn more about <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-patient-responsibility-benefits" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">patient costs</a>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The family unit number. This is returned when the payer is a pharmacy benefits manager (PBM) and the patient has a suffix to their member ID number that is used in the NCPDP Telecom Standard Insurance Segment, in field <code>303-C3</code> (Person Code). For all other uses, the family unit number (suffix) is considered part of the patient&#x27;s member ID number.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].benefitsAdditionalInformation.hicNumber</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The health insurance claim number (HICN). Note that CMS previously used the HICN to uniquely identify Medicare beneficiaries. However, they have since transitioned to a new, randomized Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) format. The HICN is no longer used for Medicare transactions but this property is now used by some payers to return MBI. If you receive a value in this property that matches the format specified in the <a href="https://www.cms.gov/training-education/partner-outreach-resources/new-medicare-card/medical-beneficiary-identifiers-mbis" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Medicare Beneficiary Identifier documentation</a>, the number is likely an MBI and we recommend sending a follow-up eligibility check to CMS for additional benefits data. This most commonly occurs with patients who are covered by both Medicare and Medicaid.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M9 3V5H6V19H9V21H4V3H9ZM15 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Other entities associated with the eligibility or benefits. This could be a provider, an individual, an organization, or another payer. When present, this array typically contains information about the patient&#x27;s primary care provider (PCP), another organization that handles a specific benefit type (such as telehealth mental health services), or another health plan for the patient (coordination of benefits scenarios).</p>
<ul>
<li>This is where information for a crossover carrier such as Medicaid or Medicare is provided, if it&#x27;s applicable to the patient and the payer supports it.</li>
<li>For Blue Cross Blue Shield (BCBS) payers, Stedi returns an entry containing information about the patient&#x27;s home plan - the plan that actually verified the coverage. In this object, the <code>entityIdentifier</code> property is set to <code>Party Performing Verification</code>. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-active-coverage-benefits#bcbs-home-plan" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></li>
</ul><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The communication number referenced in <code>communicationMode</code>. It includes the country or area code when applicable.</p>
<p>Note that phone numbers are formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB represents the telephone number prefix, and CCCC represents the telephone number. Phone numbers are provided without separators, such as dashes or parentheses. For example, <code>5551123345</code> for <code>555-112-3345</code>.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code specifying the relationship between the entity and the patient. Can be <code>01</code> - Parent, <code>02</code> - Child, <code>27</code> - Domestic Partner, <code>41</code> - Spouse, <code>48</code> - Employee, <code>65</code> - Other, or <code>72</code> - Unknown.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code specifying the type of quantity for the benefit. Can be <code>DY</code> - Days, <code>FL</code> - Units, <code>HS</code> - Hours, <code>MN</code> - Month, and <code>VS</code> - Visits.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Identifies the external code list used to provide the specified procedure or service codes. Can be <code>AD</code> - American Dental Association, <code>CJ</code> - Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) codes, <code>HC</code> - Health Care Financing Administration Common Procedural Coding System (HCPCS) Codes, <code>ID</code> - International Classification of Diseases 9th Revision, Clinical Modification (ICD-9-CM) - Procedure, <code>IV</code> - Home Infusion EDI Coalition (HIEC) Product/Service Code, <code>N4</code> - National Drug Code in 5-4-2 Format, or <code>ZZ</code> - Mutually Defined</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The full name of the coverage level code.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">Expand</button></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Children Only</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Dependents Only</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Employee and Children</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Employee Only</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Employee and Spouse</code></div></div></div><div class="p-3 border-t"><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">View <!-- -->4<!-- --> more item<!-- -->s</button></div></section></div></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating the level of coverage for the patient.</p>
<p>This will either be <code>CHD</code> - Children Only, <code>DEP</code> - Dependents Only, <code>ECH</code> - Employee and Children, <code>EMP</code> - Employee Only, <code>ESP</code> - Employee and Spouse, <code>FAM</code> - Family, <code>IND</code> - Individual, <code>SPC</code> - Spouse and Children, <code>SPO</code> - Spouse Only, or <code>Unknown</code>.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 first:border-t-0"><div><code>ECH</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>EMP</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>ESP</code></div></div></div><div class="p-3 border-t"><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">View <!-- -->4<!-- --> class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The code category. Always set to <code>44</code> - Nature of Injury.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 For example <code>Mutually Defined</code> when the code is set to <code>ZZ</code>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].eligibilityAdditionalInformationList[].codeListQualifierCode</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Identifies a specific industry code list. Can be <code>GR</code> - National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) Nature of Injury Code, <code>NI</code> - Nature of Injury Code, or <code>ZZ</code> - Mutually Defined.</p>
<p>When this is set to <code>ZZ</code>, the <code>industryCode</code> property will be set to a place of service code.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 break-words">items[].benefitsInformation[].inPlanNetworkIndicatorCode</div><div class="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Code indicating whether the benefit is in-network or out-of-network. Can be <code>Y</code> - Yes, <code>N</code> - No, <code>U</code> - Unknown, or <code>W</code> - Not Applicable</p>
<p>Code <code>U</code> indicates that it is unknown whether the benefits are in or out-of-network. Code <code>W</code> indicates that the benefit applies to both in and out-of-network providers.</p>
<p>Note that this property <strong>doesn&#x27;t indicate</strong> whether the provider is in or out-of-network for the patient. To determine that, you must check with the payer directly.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The specific product name or special program name for an insurance plan. For example <code>Gold 1-2-3</code>.</p>
<p>Payers are normally required to send the plan name when <code>benefitsInformation.code</code> is set to values <code>1</code> - <code>8</code> and the <code>benefitsInformation.serviceTypeCodes</code> contains <code>30</code> (Health Benefit Plan Coverage). However, behavior may vary by payer, so don&#x27;t rely on this information being present in the response. Note that the plan name returned in this property may not exactly match the name the payer uses in official plan documents or marketing literature.</p>
<p>Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-active-coverage-benefits#what%E2%80%99s-the-plan-name%3F" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">What&#x27;s the plan name?</a> in the benefits response documentation for more details.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!p-3" benefit type. For example, <code>7</code> - Anesthesia. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/send-eligibility-checks#service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Service Type Codes</a> for a complete list.</p>
<p>This list is specific to X12 version 005010, which is the mandated version for eligibility checks. It differs from the current <a href="https://x12.org/codes/service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">X12 Service Type Codes</a> list, which applies to X12 versions later than 005010.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 in the <code>serviceTypeCodes</code> array. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/send-eligibility-checks#service-type-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Service Type Codes</a> for a complete list of codes and their names.</p>
<p>The word physician in service type codes refers to any healthcare provider, including physician assistants, nurse practitioners, and other types of healthcare professionals.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The member&#x27;s government service affiliation code, used to report military service data. <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">Expand</button></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>A</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>B</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t <p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 Spouse, <code>19</code> - Child, <code>20</code> Employee, <code>21</code> - Unknown, <code>39</code> - Organ Donor, <code>40</code> - Cadaver Donor, <code>53</code> - Life Partner, or <code>G8</code> - Other Relationship.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information about the entity that submitted the original eligibility check request. This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider. This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider&#x27;s <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">NPI</a>, tax ID, or EIN.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative 3H20V21H15V19H18V5H15V3Z"></path></svg>Array of Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>provider</code> level include missing or incorrect information and issues with the provider&#x27;s NPI registration with the payer. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Information to help you correct the error.</p>
<p>We periodically update this guidance, so these strings may change at any time and may differ between eligibility responses. <strong>Don&#x27;t build programmatic logic that depends on matching class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The payer&#x27;s contact information.</p>
<p>Note that when <code>contacts.communicationMode</code> is set to <code>UR</code>, the <code>communicationNumber</code> property may not contain a valid URL. Most payers provide a partial web address for their provider portal, or something similar, such as <code>www.example.com/portal</code>. You must add the appropriate scheme and separators, such as <code>https://</code> or <code>http://</code>, to make it a valid URL.</p><details pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The communication number referenced in <code>communicationMode</code>. It includes the country or area code when applicable.</p>
<p>Note that phone numbers are formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB represents the telephone number prefix, and CCCC represents the telephone number. Phone numbers are provided without separators, such as dashes or parentheses. For example, <code>5551123345</code> for <code>555-112-3345</code>.</p></div></div></div></details></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card 5.44772 17.5523 5 17 5H16V3H17C18.6569 3 20 4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>Object</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Contains the dates associated with coverage for this health plan. This information can help you determine the patient&#x27;s eligibility for benefits.
- All dates are formatted as YYYYMMDD (for single dates) or as YYYYMMDD-YYYYMMDD (for date ranges).
- Properties contain a single date unless otherwise noted.
- Most payers return either <code>plan</code> or <code>planBegin</code> and <code>planEnd</code>, but the exact dates returned depend on the payer&#x27;s discretion and the specific health plan.
- If the date of service is after the earliest ending <code>plan</code>, <code>eligibility</code>, <code>planEnd</code>, <code>eligibilityEnd</code>, <code>policyEffective</code>, or <code>policyExpiration</code> value, the patient likely doesn&#x27;t have active coverage.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" data-path="response.items.planDateInformation" id="response.items.planDateInformation"><summary items-center gap-1 mt-3"><span class="px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md font-[500] text-[13px] bg-blue-50 dark:bg-blue-950/50 text-blue-700 dark:text-blue-300 break-all whitespace-normal inline-flex items-center gap-1"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="24" height="24" fill="currentColor" class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The health insurance claim number (HICN). Note that CMS previously used the HICN to uniquely identify Medicare beneficiaries. However, they have since transitioned to a new, randomized Medicare Beneficiary Identifier (MBI) format. The HICN is no longer used for Medicare transactions but this property is now used by some payers to return MBI. If you receive a value in this property that matches the format specified in the <a href="https://www.cms.gov/training-education/partner-outreach-resources/new-medicare-card/medical-beneficiary-identifiers-mbis" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Medicare Beneficiary Identifier documentation</a>, the number is likely an MBI and we recommend sending a follow-up eligibility check to CMS for additional benefits data. This most commonly occurs with patients who are covered by both Medicare and Medicaid.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none last:border-b overflow-visible [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!border-none [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!p-0 [role=tabpanel]_&amp;:!rounded-none [role=tabpanel]_details_&amp;:!border-t to report military service data. Visit <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-code-lists#information-status-codes" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Eligibility code lists</a> for a complete list.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 This is always <code>25</code></p>

It can also be included when the payer used the birth sequence number from the original request to locate the subscriber in their system. This is always <code>001</code></p>

This may be an individual practitioner, a medical group, a hospital, or another type of healthcare provider. This object will always include at least one identifier, such as the provider&#x27;s <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/national-provider-identifier" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">NPI</a>, tax ID, or EIN.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>When a payer rejects your eligibility check, the response contains one or more <code>AAA</code> errors that specify the reasons for the rejection and any recommended follow-up actions. Common reasons for rejection at the <code>provider</code> level include missing or incorrect information and issues with the provider&#x27;s NPI registration with the payer. <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/eligibility-troubleshooting#payer-aaa-errors" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Learn more</a></p><details pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Allowed actions you can take, based on the rejection reason code. For example <code>Please Correct and Resubmit</code>.</p>
<p>Payers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div><button class="text-sm text-fd-primary/60 hover:text-fd-primary/80 cursor-pointer font-medium">Expand</button></div><div class="flex flex-col max-h-[700px] overflow-y-auto"><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Please Correct and Resubmit</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Resubmission Not Allowed</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Resubmission Allowed</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Do Not Resubmit; Inquiry Initiated to a Third Party</code></div></div><div class="flex flex-col gap-1 p-3 border-t first:border-t-0"><div><code>Please Wait 30 Days and Resubmit</code></div></div></div><div class="p-3 class="remixicon size-3.5"><path d="M3 4H21V6H3V4ZM3 19H17V21H3V19ZM3 14H21V16H3V14ZM3 9H17V11H3V9Z"></path></svg>String</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The status of the discovery check. This is either <code>PENDING</code> or <code>COMPLETE</code>.
- If the status is <code>COMPLETE</code>, the <code>items</code> array will contain any potential coverage matches Stedi found for the patient.
- If the status is <code>PENDING</code>, the check is still in progress. You can immediately begin polling the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/get-insurance-discovery-results" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Insurance Discovery Check Results</a> endpoint to retrieve the results asynchronously.</p><div><section class="rounded-md border bg-fd-card"><div class="flex flex-wrap justify-between p-3 border-t border-b first:border-t-0 last:rounded-b-md first:rounded-t-md"><div aria-level="1" class="text-sm text-fd-primary font-medium" role="heading">Possible values</div></div><div class="flex flex-row"><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>PENDING</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>COMPLETE</code></div><div class="px-3 my-3 border-r last:border-r-0"><code>ERROR</code></div></div></section></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div class="hidden xl:flex xl:flex-col self-start sticky gap-6 max-w-[28rem] 8)"><pre class="min-w-full w-max *:flex *:flex-col shiki stedi-custom" style="background-color:#0A0A0A;color:#D4D4D4" tabindex="0"><code><span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">{</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">  &quot;discoveryId&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;12345678-abcd-4321-efgh-987654321abc&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">  &quot;items&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">    {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">      &quot;benefitsInformation&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">        {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;additionalInformation&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">            {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">              &quot;description&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;To determine if a prior authorization is required, please check the health plan&#x27;s website.&quot;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">            }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">          ],</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;benefitsRelatedEntities&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">            {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">              &quot;entityFirstname&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Jane&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">              &quot;entityIdentification&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;XX&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">              &quot;entityIdentificationValue&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;1234567890&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">              &quot;entityIdentifier&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Primary Care Provider&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">              &quot;entityName&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Dough&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">              &quot;entityType&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Person&quot;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">            }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">          ],</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;code&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;1&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;inPlanNetworkIndicator&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Not Applicable&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;inPlanNetworkIndicatorCode&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;W&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;name&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Active Coverage&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;planCoverage&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Gold Plan&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;serviceTypeCodes&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#CA8A04">            &quot;30&quot;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">          ],</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;serviceTypes&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#CA8A04">            &quot;Health Benefit Plan Coverage&quot;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">          ]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">        },</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">        {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;code&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;1&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;inPlanNetworkIndicator&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Not Applicable&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;inPlanNetworkIndicatorCode&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;W&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;name&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Active Coverage&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;planCoverage&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#CA8A04">&quot;Gold Plan&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#3B82F6">          &quot;serviceTypeCodes&quot;</span><span style="color:#D4D4D4">: [</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#CA8A04">            &quot;1&quot;</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#D4D4D4">          ],</span></span>

We especially recommend providing the dependent's Social Security Number and their address - particularly their zip code.\\n- You should provide information for both the subscriber and the dependent in the request when possible.\\n- If you only have the dependent's information, you should identify them in the `subscriber` object instead and leave this object empty. Note that some payers require information about both the dependent and the subscriber, so providing only the dependent's information limits Stedi's ability to return coverage matches for those payers.\",\"properties\":{\"address\":{\"description\":\"The dependent's address. We strongly recommend providing this information if possible to improve the probability of finding matching coverage.\\n\\nWe especially recommend providing the patient’s ZIP code, as this helps narrow down the list of probable payers. Zip code search isn’t an exact match, so even the first 3-4 digits of the patient’s current zip code can help improve the results. If the patient’s current address isn’t available, you can try a full or partial zip code from one of the patient’s previous addresses or even one in close proximity.\",\"properties\":{\"address1\":{\"description\":\"The first line of the address.\",\"maxLength\":55,\"minLength\":1,\"type\":\"string\"},\"address2\":{\"description\":\"The second line of the address.\",\"maxLength\":55,\"minLength\":1,\"type\":\"string\"},\"city\":{\"description\":\"The city.\",\"maxLength\":30,\"minLength\":2,\"type\":\"string\"},\"countryCode\":{\"description\":\"The two-letter country code from [Part 1 of ISO 3166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2).\",\"maxLength\":2,\"minLength\":2,\"type\":\"string\"},\"countrySubDivisionCode\":{\"description\":\"The country subdivision code from [Part 2 of ISO 3166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-2).\",\"maxLength\":3,\"minLength\":1,\"type\":\"string\"},\"postalCode\":{\"description\":\"The United States or Canadian postal code, excluding punctuation and blanks.\",\"maxLength\":9,\"minLength\":5,\"type\":\"string\"},\"state\":{\"description\":\"The US state or Canadian province code with unknown option. For example `Please Correct and Resubmit`.\\n\\nPayers may sometimes return other non-compliant values.\",\"enum\":[\"Please Correct and Resubmit\",\"Resubmission Not Allowed\",\"Please Resubmit Original Transaction\",\"Resubmission Allowed\",\"Do Not Resubmit; Inquiry Initiated to a Third Party\",\"Please Wait 30 Days and Resubmit\",\"Please Wait 10 Days and Resubmit\",\"Do Not Resubmit; We
